trigger_keywords: what are the zero field names
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The values 'vector_1_zero=yes' and 'vector_2_zero=yes' indicate that specific vector indices have been fully set to zero status.

trigger_keywords: test cases for feature2 zero vector detection
show me feature2 zero vector detection test cases
what are the feature2 zero vector detection inputs and outputs
give me the feature2_zero_vector_detection test file
feature2 zero vector detection expected output

context_pointers.vague_reference: refer to the 'headers' module for position logic
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The position logic for words matches the same ordering convention found in the 'headers' feature, where indices are strictly 0-based and insertion order is preserved.
The output format for top/second best categories follows the exact same structure and delimiter usage as the ranking results seen in semantic document similarity lists.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_token_frequency.json.description: Counts meaningful terms in text after configurable punctuation filtering, stopword removal, and optional stemming.

input.text: here are some good words of test's. I hope you love them!

cases.expected_output:
!=1
'=1
.=1
good=1
hope=1
love=1
test=1
them=1
word=1

input.text: here are some good words of test's. I hope you love them!

cases.expected_output:
good=1
hope=1
love=1
test=1
them=1
word=1

input.text: here are some good words of test's. I hope you love them!

cases.expected_output:
good=1
hope=1
love=1
tests=1
them=1
words=1

feature2_zero_vector_detection.json.description: Reports whether numeric vectors are zero vectors, including empty vectors and vectors containing only zero values.

cases.expected_output:
vector_0_length=0
vector_0_zero=yes
vector_1_length=1
vector_1_zero=yes
vector_2_length=10
vector_2_zero=yes

feature3_matrix_singular_decomposition.j.description: Decomposes a numeric matrix and reports the dimensions of decomposition outputs plus rounded singular values.

cases.expected_output:
input_rows=2
input_columns=2
left_rows=2
left_columns=2
right_rows=2
right_columns=2
singular_values=1.00,1.00

feature4_ordered_word_vocabulary.json.description: Maintains an ordered unique vocabulary where duplicate additions do not increase size and positions follow first insertion order.

cases.expected_output:
size_after_0=1
size_after_1=1
size_after_2=2
position_hello=0
position_world=1
word_at_0=hello
word_at_1=world

feature5_1_probabilistic_training_catego.description: Trains a probabilistic text classifier, returns accepted training tokens, and reports category state before and after adding a category.

training.text: love
Água

cases.expected_output:
stemmer=enabled
initial_categories=Interesting,Uninteresting
trained_Interesting=love
trained_Interesting=Água
final_categories=Interesting,Test,Uninteresting

feature5_2_dynamic_category_handling.jso.description: Handles training for categories that were not declared at creation time according to the configured dynamic-category policy.

training.text: A really sweet language

cases.expected_output:
categories=Interesting,Ruby,Uninteresting
category_count=3

training.text: A really sweet language

cases.expected_output:
error=category_not_found
category=Ruby

feature5_3_probabilistic_classification..description: Classifies text after supervised training and exposes both predicted category and rounded numeric confidence score.

training.text: here are some good words. I hope you love them
here are some bad words, I hate you

queries: I hate bad words and you

cases.expected_output:
query_0_category=Uninteresting
query_0_score=-4.85

feature5_4_threshold_classification.json.description: Applies an enabled confidence threshold so familiar inputs classify to the trained category and out-of-distribution inputs return no category.

cases.expected_output:
one=Number
two=Number
three=Number
four=Number
five=Number
xyzzy=none

feature5_5_untraining_changes_prediction.description: Removes previously learned evidence and reports that the prediction for an affected query changes after untraining.

training.text: here are some good words. I hope you love them
here are some bad words, I hate you
red orange green blue seven

cases.expected_output:
before=Colors
after=Uninteresting

feature5_6_custom_stopwords.json.description: Uses caller-supplied stopwords to skip all-stopword training text while allowing other text to train and affect classification scores.

training.text: Custom stopwords
To be or not to be

queries: To be or not to be

cases.expected_output:
categories=Stopwords
query_0_category=Stopwords
query_0_score=infinity
query_1_category=Stopwords
query_1_score=0.00

feature6_1_semantic_related_documents.js.description: Indexes documents semantically and returns the most related documents for a query in ranked order.

documents: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.
input.query: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.

cases.expected_output:
rank_0=This text involves dogs too. Dogs! 
rank_1=This text involves birds. Birds.
rank_2=This text revolves around cats. Cats.

feature6_2_semantic_classification_score.description: Assigns semantic categories to query documents and reports rounded scores for each classification.

training.text: This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.

queries: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text revolves around cats. Cats.

cases.expected_output:
query_0_category=Dog
query_0_score=2.49
query_1_category=Cat
query_1_score=1.41
query_2_category=Bird
query_2_score=2.00

feature6_3_semantic_scored_categories.js.description: Ranks semantic categories related to a mixed query and reports only categories with relevant evidence.

documents: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.

cases.expected_output:
result_count=2
rank_0=Bird:0.67
rank_1=Dog:0.69

feature6_4_semantic_context_classificati.description: Demonstrates that semantic classification can prefer contextual subject matter over a purely token-probability classifier on the same training set.

training.text: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.

cases.expected_output:
semantic_category=Dog
probabilistic_category=Cat

feature6_5_semantic_recategorization.jso.description: Allows categories attached to existing indexed documents to be changed without rebuilding the index, changing later classifications.

documents: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.

recategorize.text: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!

cases.expected_output:
before=Dog
needs_rebuild=no
after=Cow

feature6_6_semantic_search_ranking.json.description: Searches indexed documents by query text and returns ranked document matches for phrase and keyword queries.

documents: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.

cases.expected_output:
query_0=dog involves
query_0_rank_0=This text involves dogs too. Dogs! 
query_0_rank_1=This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
query_0_rank_2=This text also involves cats. Cats!
query_0_rank_3=This text involves birds. Birds.
query_0_rank_4=This text revolves around cats. Cats.
query_1=dog
query_1_rank_0=This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
query_1_rank_1=This text involves dogs too. Dogs! 
query_1_rank_2=This text also involves cats. Cats!
query_1_rank_3=This text involves birds. Birds.
query_1_rank_4=This text revolves around cats. Cats.

feature6_7_serialized_index_consistency..description: Serializes and restores a semantic index while preserving ranked search results.

documents: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.

cases.expected_output:
original_rank_0=This text revolves around cats. Cats.
original_rank_1=This text also involves cats. Cats!
original_rank_2=This text involves dogs too. Dogs! 
restored_rank_0=This text revolves around cats. Cats.
restored_rank_1=This text also involves cats. Cats!
restored_rank_2=This text involves dogs too. Dogs!

feature6_8_keywords_and_summary.json.description: Extracts highest-ranked keyword stems from a document and summarizes a document collection into a fixed number of representative sentences.

documents: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
This text involves dogs too. Dogs!
This text revolves around cats. Cats.
This text also involves cats. Cats!
This text involves birds. Birds.
input.keyword_source: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.
input.summary_source: This text deals with dogs. Dogs.This text involves dogs too. Dogs! This text revolves around cats. Cats.This text also involves cats. Cats!This text involves birds. Birds.

cases.expected_output:
keywords=dog,text,deal
summary=This text involves dogs too [...] This text also involves cats

feature6_9_document_validation_warning.j.description: Warns in normalized form when an added document contains only stopwords or words too short to produce usable terms.

cases.expected_output:
warning=empty_document
document=i can

_constraints.input_condition: include_punctuation is false and stem is true
_constraints.output_rule: Must remove all punctuation characters from tokens

_constraints.input_condition: include_punctuation is true and stem is true
_constraints.output_rule: Must retain punctuation characters as separate tokens, count them, and ignore stopwords

_constraints.input_condition: stem is false
_constraints.output_rule: Must NOT apply stemming; output should include full forms of words ending in 's' or other inflections

_constraints.input_condition: Any valid input
_constraints.output_rule: Output lines must be sorted lexically (alphabetical) by the token text

_constraints.input_condition: include_punctuation is false and stem is true
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be sorted by the original token text, but stems are replaced by their base form for sorting/duplication logic

_constraints.input_condition: Vector is empty array []
_constraints.output_rule: Output length as 0, zero status as yes

_constraints.input_condition: Vector contains only zeros
_constraints.output_rule: Output length matching array size, zero status as yes

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a square matrix
_constraints.output_rule: Output left_rows, left_columns, right_rows, right_columns equal to the matrix dimensions

_constraints.input_condition: Any valid numeric matrix
_constraints.output_rule: Singular values must be formatted with exactly two decimal places (e.g., 1.00)

_constraints.input_condition: Duplicate word added to 'words' array
_constraints.output_rule: Output size_after_N must remain unchanged from previous addition

_constraints.input_condition: First occurrence of a word added
_constraints.output_rule: Output size_after_N increments by 1

_constraints.input_condition: Lookup or Index for a valid position
_constraints.output_rule: Position must return the index (0-based) where the word was first inserted

_constraints.input_condition: add_category field is present
_constraints.output_rule: Final categories list must include the newly added category even if it wasn't in initial_categories

_constraints.input_condition: Training sample provided
_constraints.output_rule: trained_<Category> output lines must list each token from the training text

_constraints.input_condition: Query text provided
_constraints.output_rule: Output must have category and score on separate lines for each query

_constraints.input_condition: Query contains keywords that strongly indicate one category
_constraints.output_rule: Score must be rounded to two decimal places

_constraints.input_condition: threshold is negative number (default) and query matches training terms
_constraints.output_rule: Output format should be 'word=Category'

_constraints.input_condition: Query does not meet confidence threshold
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be 'word=none'

_constraints.input_condition: Document is added to training, then to untraining, and is queried
_constraints.output_rule: Output must report 'before' and 'after' predictions on separate lines

_constraints.input_condition: Query matches only the untrained portion of a category
_constraints.output_rule: Category prediction must switch to the other category with remaining evidence

_constraints.input_condition: Training text consists entirely of custom stopwords
_constraints.output_rule: The category must be created, but query score should reflect no positive evidence (infinity for perfect match if defined, or low score)

_constraints.input_condition: Query contains custom stopwords mixed with trained terms
_constraints.output_rule: Query must still classify, score depends on ratio of non-stopword terms to total terms

_constraints.input_condition: Query matches multiple documents equally
_constraints.output_rule: Rank order should be deterministic and stable

_constraints.input_condition: Query matches multiple categories
_constraints.output_rule: Output must list top category with score on one line and second best on next

_constraints.input_condition: Query matches multiple categories with similar scores
_constraints.output_rule: Output must rank categories by score and limit to top N (default 2)

_constraints.input_condition: Query text context differs from pure token probability
_constraints.output_rule: Output must show both 'semantic_category' and 'probabilistic_category'

_constraints.input_condition: documents are indexed and then recategorized
_constraints.output_rule: Output must report 'needs_rebuild=no' indicating index update is supported

_constraints.input_condition: Document category is changed
_constraints.output_rule: Change must be reflected in future 'after' query result

_constraints.input_condition: Index is serialized and then deserialized
_constraints.output_rule: Query results must match exactly before and after serialization

_constraints.input_condition: Document contains only stopwords or is very short
_constraints.output_rule: Output must begin with 'warning=empty_document'

_constraints.input_condition: Words are added in order
_constraints.output_rule: Word at index N must be the N-th word added to the vocabulary

_constraints.input_condition: auto_categorize is false and new category attempted
_constraints.output_rule: Output format for error must include 'category=X'

_constraints.input_condition: auto_categorize is true
_constraints.output_rule: New category is added to the list and count increments

_constraints.input_condition: Matrix has multiple singular values
_constraints.output_rule: Values must be comma-separated

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple queries provided
_constraints.output_rule: Each query result must be prefixed with 'query_<N>_' where N is 0-based index

_constraints.input_condition: Search results returned for multiple queries
_constraints.output_rule: Each document in results ranked for a query must be prefixed with query index

_constraints.input_condition: sentence_count is 2
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain exactly 2 sentences from the document source

_constraints.input_condition: keyword_source text provided
_constraints.output_rule: Output must list top N stemmed keywords separated by commas
